rulings.Case Title: REBECCA BROWN VS. FAMILY SERVICE AGENCY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, Compel Responses To Discovery, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 18, 2012, LINE 1, DEFENDANT FAMILY SERVICE AGENCY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED, COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, AND FOR SANCTIONS. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff has failed to respond to any of the discovery propounded by Defendant Family Service Agency of San Francisco that is the subject of Defendant's motion, and has thus waived all objections to the discovery. All of the requests for admission in Defendant Family Service Agency of San Francisco's Requests for Admission, Set One (consisting of Requests for Admission Nos. 1 through 22) are DEEMED ADMITTED. Plaintiff shall provide written responses, without objection, to Defendant's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production, Set One no later than June 29th. Defendant's requests for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant the sum of $1,640 by June 29th. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALEJANDRO MARRON VS. ONEIL CONSTRUCTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Of Emmett Mcmahon Dba Mcmahon Construction O'Neill Construction'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 18, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT EMMET MCMAHON DBA MCMAHON CONSTRUCTION'S MOTION Of Emmett Mcmahon Dba Mcmahon Construction O'Neill Construction'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement-The motion to contest O'Neill's application for good faith settlement is denied. Substantial evidence supports the finding that the settlement is in the ballpark under Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., 38 Cal.3d 488 (1985) and the objector fails to show that the settlement lacks good faith. See CCP 877.6(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JM

rulings.Case Title: TBF FINANCIAL, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. IMMUNE ENHANCEMENT PROJECT, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 18, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF TBF FINANCIAL, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Adam Boehm are sustained and thus Plaintiff has failed to meet its burden of production. Even if all of the Boehm declaration is considered, the purported assignment contains no mention of the Financing Agreement. Plaintiff's untimely attempt to correct this omission fails because the newly added document refers to the Financing Agreement as File # 907-0000938-000, a number that appears nowhere on the Financing Agreement. The Financing Agreement repeatedly refers to itself as Agreement #606838. Finally, even if Plaintiff did meet its burden of production, Defendant raised a triable issue of material fact as to when payments on the Financing Agreement stopped and whether the Defendant's executive director was authorized to make the purchase. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JM

rulings.Case Title: AARON FERGUSON et al VS. W. B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 18, 2012, Line 7. DEFENDANTS W. COYLE AND TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs have failed to plead the existence of a fiduciary duty owed by Telegraph Hill to these individual Plaintiffs. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action to plead facts showing the identity and capacity of the person or persons who made statements, the specific statements made and to whom the representations were made. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: AARON FERGUSON et al VS. W. B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 18, 2012, Line 7. DEFENDANTS W. COYLE AND TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike the prayer for attorneys' fees (page 15, 10) is granted without leave to amend but without prejudice to Plaintiffs filing a motion for leave to amend to add such prayer if they later uncover a basis for such prayer. The motion to strike the alter ego allegations (page 9, 46-49, page 10, 50, and page 15, 9) is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to state facts showing how or why failure to recognize the alter ego would lead to an inequitable result. The motion to strike the prayer for recovery from the Real Estate Broker's Recovery Fund (page 13, 63 and page 14, 5) is granted without leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JJM)
